text.title: Pair Programming Best Practices
text.summary: Environment setup, Pomodoro time management, communication patterns, skill-level pairings, and remote tools.

rule.statement: Define the session mode at the start (classic, ping-pong, strong-style, or tour-guide). Do not switch modes mid-session without resetting context.
rule.rationale: Mode ambiguity creates role confusion; explicitly naming the mode aligns both participants on expectations.

rule.statement: Use Pomodoro: 25-minute focused blocks, 5-minute breaks. Switch roles at breaks or natural stopping points. Max 4-6 hours of pairing per day.
rule.rationale: Continuous pairing without breaks causes mental exhaustion, especially remote. The agent does not tire but the human does.

rule.statement: Keep prompts short when working with an AI navigator. Paste diffs and test output, not full files.
rule.rationale: Full files consume context budget and force the agent to re-read code it already knows. Diffs and test output provide targeted feedback.

section.title: Expert + Novice
section.p: Use Tour Guide or Strong-Style. Expert navigates more often. Novice drives to learn by doing. Expert must explain why, not just what.

section.title: Expert + Expert
section.p: High productivity. Watch for keyboard hogging. Take breaks to avoid fatigue. Good for complex problems.

section.title: Novice + Novice
section.p: Longer sessions needed. Focus on learning, not speed. Get expert review after the session.

list.item: VS Code Live Share — full IDE sharing, free, cross-platform

rule.statement: Run a silent retro at the end of each session: agent generates a 5-bullet summary, human edits it. Persist to a session.md file for next session context.
rule.rationale: Agents reset context between sessions. session.md bridges the gap and ensures continuity across multiple pairing sessions.
